title: Pedilonum montigena
taxonomy: Pedilonum montigena is classified within the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it in the family Rubiaceae, tribe Psychotrieae, and the subfamily Rubioideae. Within the subfamily Rubioideae, it is further classified into the tribe Psychotrieae and the subtribe Palicoureeae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Pedilonum montigena, also known as the Philippine pedilonum or montigena, is a plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which make it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, infections, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Pedilonum montigena include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
